preamble: The close front vowel /i/ lowers to the close-mid front vowel [e] through emphasis spreading in Moroccan Arabic and other dialect contexts, where high vowels lower in the vicinity of emphatic consonants. This process reflects vowel lowering as a coarticulation effect in emphatic consonant environments.

examples.note: High front vowel lowers to mid-front due to emphasis spreading from adjacent emphatic consonants (tˤ, dˤ, sˤ, zˤ, qˤ)
Emphasis spreading from emphatic context causes lowering of /i/ in Moroccan dialects
Atonic vowel lowering in early Romance languages, systematic reduction of unstressed high vowels
